Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Why use Unity for slot game development instead of HTML5 or a proprietary engine?

Unity's primary advantage over HTML5 for regulated casino slot development is the separation of concerns it enables through its component-based architecture and ScriptableObject system. In an HTML5 slot built on a JavaScript framework, the RNG, game logic, and UI are typically executed in the same JavaScript thread — which can make the RNG implementation difficult to isolate for ITL review. Unity's C# architecture allows the CSPRNG module to be compiled, tested, and submitted as a completely independent component. For cross-platform deployment, Unity's IL2CPP compilation also produces significantly better performance on mobile hardware than transpiled JavaScript, which matters for premium 3D slot titles. Compared to proprietary engines, Unity's 40–60% faster development timeline advantage is real, driven by a mature ecosystem of battle-tested iGaming-specific plugins, documentation, and specialist developer availability.

Q2: What is a ScriptableObject and why does it matter for a Unity slot game?

A ScriptableObject is a Unity data container that exists as an asset independent of any scene or GameObject. In a Unity slot game, Sudonex.com uses ScriptableObjects to store all game configuration data — payout tables, symbol weights, bonus trigger probabilities, RTP settings, volatility parameters, and jurisdiction-specific rule configurations. The key benefit for casino operators is that changing the game's maths configuration for a different jurisdiction (different RTP, different bonus buy availability) requires only swapping the ScriptableObject data asset — not modifying any game code. This means jurisdiction-specific variants of the same certified game can be maintained without triggering a full ITL recertification, provided the core game code remains unchanged.

Q3: How long does it take to get a Unity slot game certified by an ITL?

The ITL review process itself typically takes 4 to 8 weeks from submission, depending on the laboratory's current queue and the complexity of the submission. The most common cause of extended timelines is a failed initial submission — requiring a resubmission cycle that adds 4 to 8 weeks per cycle. Sudonex.com's pre-certification QA process, which includes AI-driven simulation across one billion rounds and a full internal source code review using the ITL's own checklist, is specifically designed to resolve the most common certification failure causes before submission. Sudonex.com's pre-certification QA reduces the average resubmission rate to near zero on standard game builds, which typically means a total certification timeline of 4 to 8 weeks from submission rather than 3 to 6 months for operators working with studios that submit without pre-certification QA.

Q4: Can a Unity slot game operate on a land-based cabinet?

Yes. Unity's IL2CPP compilation target supports custom executable builds for the Windows and Linux operating systems used by the majority of modern land-based gaming cabinet hardware. Sudonex.com has experience building Unity slot games for cabinet deployment, including kiosk UI mode implementation (no title bar, no system cursor, locked full-screen), NVRAM integration for critical game state preservation (power-loss recovery), hardware button input mapping, and the cabinet-specific ITL certification requirements that differ from the online slot certification process. Land-based cabinet deployment requires a separate hardware certification process alongside the game software certification — Sudonex.com manages this process in coordination with the cabinet manufacturer's technical team.

Q5: Does Sudonex.com handle the Unity Gambling License requirement?

Unity's Gambling License is a licensing agreement required by Unity Technologies for any game that involves real-money gambling mechanics and is distributed through Unity-powered channels. Sudonex.com advises all clients on the Unity Gambling License requirement at the commercial brief stage and assists with the licence application process as part of the project scope. The licence requirement applies differently depending on the distribution channel — games distributed via an operator's own website or proprietary platform typically require a specific Unity licence tier, while games distributed through aggregator networks may have different requirements. Sudonex.com's legal and compliance team ensures that the Unity licensing position is correctly established before development begins, preventing mid-project complications that could affect the launch timeline.

Why Sudonex.com Is the Right Unity Slot Game Development Partner

Unity powers 70% of new mobile games in 2026. What it does not do automatically is make every game built on it commercially successful. The difference between a Unity slot game that passes ITL certification on the first submission and generates sustainable GGR for its operator, and one that costs twice its development budget in resubmission cycles and player churn, is the technical foundation: a CSPRNG-compliant RNG module, a ScriptableObject-based maths architecture that certifiers can review in isolation, a URP visual pipeline optimised for the actual hardware your players are using, and an AI-driven QA process that validates the maths model before any regulator sees it.

Sudonex.com's Unity slot game development service is built around those technical foundations. The commercial brief shapes the creative and commercial direction. The GDD and maths specification lock the scope before production begins. The modular C# architecture ensures every system can be built, tested, and certified independently. The AI simulation suite validates the maths model before submission. And the ITL certification process is managed by a team that has been through it enough times to know exactly what certifiers look for — and how to prevent the defects that cause delays.

Contact Sudonex.com's Unity slot development team to schedule your commercial brief today.

Game Portfolio: Traffic Games vs. Profit Games

A critical insight most white label casino software providers overlook: not all games serve the same business purpose. Sudonex.com categorises the game portfolio across two strategic functions:

Traffic Games — Acquisition and Engagement Drivers

High-frequency, low-margin games that attract volume and session length. These anchor the lobby and drive daily active users:

•       Video Slots — Cinematic 3D titles from studios like NetEnt, Pragmatic Play, and Evolution Gaming with high RTP and bonus features that drive extended sessions

•       Crash Games — High-volatility multiplier games (Aviator, JetX) that generate viral social sharing and high repeat-play frequency

•       Dice and Plinko — Physics-based probability games with player-controlled risk settings, generating high bet volume per session

•       Live Dealer Tables — Roulette, Blackjack, and Baccarat with real human dealers; anchor players who would otherwise churn to physical venues

Profit Games — Maximum Margin Revenue Generators

Lower-volume, higher-margin games that drive NGR efficiency and protect the house edge:

•       Progressive Jackpot Slots — Networked prize pools that drive acquisition while preserving operator margins on non-jackpot spins

•       Poker Rooms — Tournament rake and cash game rake generate consistent margin with no house-vs-player risk

•       Virtual Sports — Simulated events with rapid settlement and 24/7 availability, generating high bet frequency during off-peak hours

•       Specialty Games — Keno, Bingo, Lottery-style games with favourable house edges and high replay rates

Understanding the GGR vs. NGR Share Model

GGR (Gross Gaming Revenue) is the total amount wagered minus winnings paid out before any deductions. NGR (Net Gaming Revenue) is GGR minus bonuses, chargebacks, and payment processing fees. Most white label providers charge a percentage of GGR — which means you pay the revenue share before deducting your operational costs. Sudonex.com's Premium and Enterprise tiers shift to NGR-based arrangements, which are significantly more favourable for operators with active bonus and promotional programmes.

The no-GGR-share Enterprise model at Sudonex.com converts the relationship to a flat SaaS licensing fee. For operators projecting monthly GGR above $500,000, this model delivers materially better unit economics than any percentage-based arrangement.

Licensing Deep Dive: Curaçao vs. Malta Gaming Authority (MGA)

The choice of licensing jurisdiction is not just a legal question — it is a conversion rate question. Research consistently shows that landing pages displaying an MGA licence convert 18–24% higher than those displaying Curaçao alone. Here is what operators need to understand about both tracks:

Curaçao eGaming Licence

The most accessible entry point for global operators. Curaçao operates a master licence system: the Sudonex.com white label platform operates under a master licence, and client operators receive a sub-licence, dramatically simplifying the application process. Key characteristics:

•       Application timeline: 4–8 weeks under a sub-licence arrangement

•       Cost: Included in Sudonex.com's white label package at Standard tier and above

•       Geographic coverage: Accepts players from most global markets except the US, UK, France, Netherlands, and certain others

•       Regulatory intensity: Lower compliance burden than MGA — suitable for new operators testing market appetite

•       Best for: First-launch operators, crypto-focused platforms, emerging market entry

Malta Gaming Authority (MGA) Licence

The gold-standard licence for European operations and the benchmark for player trust conversion. An MGA licence allows operators to serve EU players and is increasingly required by tier-1 payment processors for merchant account approval. Key characteristics:

•       Application timeline: 4–6 months for direct application (Sudonex.com assists with full preparation)

•       Required documentation: Ownership diagrams, 3-year business plans, RNG certification, responsible gambling tool mandates

•       Geographic coverage: Full EU market access; widely recognised by payment processors globally

•       Regulatory intensity: High — ongoing reporting, quarterly compliance reviews, mandatory RG tools

•       Best for: Operators targeting EU markets, platforms requiring tier-1 payment processor relationships

UK Gambling Commission (UKGC)

Required for any operator serving UK-resident players. The UKGC operates one of the most stringent licensing regimes in the world, with mandatory affordability checks, enhanced due diligence on high-value players, and strict advertising restrictions. Sudonex.com assists operators with UKGC applications and builds all mandatory UKGC responsible gambling features into platforms targeting the UK market.

MiCA Compliance (EU Crypto Operators)

For operators accepting cryptocurrency wagers from EU players, the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation introduces CASP (Crypto-Asset Service Provider) licensing requirements. Sudonex.com's non-custodial wallet architecture minimises MiCA compliance obligations and assists with full CASP registration where custodial functions are required.

Payments & Merchant of Record: The Question Most Providers Won't Answer

Here is the payment question that separates serious white label casino software providers from template vendors: who is your Merchant of Record?

The Merchant of Record (MoR) is the legal entity responsible for processing player payments, handling chargebacks, managing disputes, and maintaining the payment processor relationship. In iGaming, this distinction matters enormously. Card fraud in the EU/EEA reached €1.3 billion in 2024. Payment processors require operators to demonstrate strong fraud controls, AML compliance, and dispute resolution capacity before granting merchant accounts — and they can terminate accounts without notice if chargeback ratios exceed thresholds.

Most white label providers leave the MoR responsibility with the operator — meaning you inherit payment liability on day one. Sudonex.com offers a structured MoR service for qualifying operators, functioning as the legal payment entity, managing processor relationships, and absorbing chargeback risk within agreed parameters. This is a meaningful operational advantage for new operators who have not yet established their own payment processor relationships.

Responsible Gambling Technology: AI-Powered Behavioural Monitoring

Responsible Gambling (RG) is no longer a compliance checkbox — it is a licensing prerequisite, a player trust signal, and increasingly a competitive differentiator. Sudonex.com builds the following RG infrastructure into every platform by default:

•       Deposit, loss, wagering, and time limits — configurable daily, weekly, and monthly, with mandatory cooling-off periods before limit increases take effect

•       Self-exclusion — accessible within three clicks from any page on the platform, with instant account suspension and cross-platform matching against national self-exclusion registers (GAMSTOP for UK, OASIS for Germany)

•       Time-outs — immediate play stops from 12 hours to six months, with no override capability during the selected period

•       Reality checks — configurable session time reminders that display time and money spent at player-set intervals

•       AI behavioural monitoring — real-time analysis of betting history via API to identify patterns associated with problem gambling (session escalation, loss-chasing, rapid deposit sequences) and trigger proactive RG interventions before escalation

•       Player-facing dashboards — full transparency on spend, session time, win/loss history, and active limits, accessible from the player account at all times

MGA and UKGC both mandate that self-exclusion must be reachable within three clicks from anywhere on the platform. Sudonex.com builds to this specification as a minimum standard on every deployment.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: How much does a white label casino software provider charge in 2026?

Costs range from $10,000–$20,000 (Basic setup) to $150,000+ (Enterprise/no-revenue-share). Monthly fees run $2,000–$20,000 depending on tier. Traditional white label arrangements also include an ongoing GGR or NGR share of 10%–30%. Sudonex.com's Enterprise tier converts to a flat SaaS fee with zero GGR share — significantly more profitable for high-volume operators. All packages include a full cost-breakdown quote before any commitment.

Q2: How long does it take to launch with a white label casino software provider?

A white label casino built on the Sudonex.com platform can go live in 2–4 months, including Curaçao sub-licence processing. The critical path is typically the licence application and payment processor merchant account approval, not the technical build. MGA direct licence applications require 4–6 months. Sudonex.com can launch a soft-open beta environment in 4–6 weeks while licensing completes.

Q3: What is the difference between a white label casino and a turnkey casino?

A white label casino operates under the provider's existing licence — the operator gets a branded sub-licence, which is faster and cheaper but involves ongoing revenue sharing and limits on customisation. A turnkey casino provides the full technology stack for the operator to deploy under their own independently-held licence. No ongoing GGR share, full UI/UX control, but higher upfront cost and longer setup time. Sudonex.com offers both models, and can transition operators from white label to turnkey as their business scales.

Q4: Who is responsible for payment processing and chargebacks on a white label platform?

This is one of the most critical questions to ask any white label casino software provider. On most platforms, the operator assumes Merchant of Record (MoR) status and is fully liable for chargebacks, payment disputes, and payment processor compliance. Sudonex.com offers a structured MoR service for qualifying operators, where Sudonex.com assumes the payment liability and manages processor relationships — removing a significant operational risk from the operator, particularly important given that card fraud in the EU/EEA reached €1.3 billion in 2024.

Q5: Can I launch a white label casino that accepts cryptocurrency?

Yes. Sudonex.com's platform includes native blockchain wallet integration (MetaMask, Trust Wallet, WalletConnect), support for BTC, ETH, USDT, SOL, BNB, and 20+ other cryptocurrencies, and fiat-to-crypto on-ramp tooling via MoonPay and Transak. For EU-facing crypto platforms, Sudonex.com also assists with MiCA CASP compliance — the EU's crypto-asset service provider licensing framework that came into full force in 2026. A non-custodial wallet architecture minimises MiCA obligations while still delivering a fully functional crypto payment experience.

The iGaming market in 2026 does not wait. The opportunity window in emerging markets, the growth of crypto gambling, and the consolidation of player loyalty around established brands all mean that time-to-market is a genuine competitive variable — and that choosing the right white label casino software provider is a strategic decision, not just a procurement one.

Sudonex.com delivers the complete package: a turnkey iGaming platform with 10,000+ games, MGA/Curaçao/UKGC licensing support, AI-powered player retention, crypto-native payment architecture, Merchant of Record services, and a transparent pricing model that includes a no-GGR-share path for serious operators.
